name: Christian Identity Provider Platform Access Map
public_identifier_policy: Public Worker and GitHub config show only the primary DNS-domain identity. Alternate personal identity-provider account identifiers stay in private Notion/Entra review notes or local-only config.
access_rule: All external identity providers are allowed only when the stable provider GUID or subject maps to the authorized member GUID for christian@thevlsc.com.

platform_targets.desired_access: Access applications, Workers, DNS, AI Gateway, and API routes for the primary DNS-domain identity.
platform_targets.current_status: Cloudflare Access IDPs available; application enforcement still pending for admin/dev routes.

platform_targets.desired_access: Repository and organization access for the primary DNS-domain identity and approved linked IDPs.
platform_targets.current_status: Local Git commit path configured; CLI push is pending local GitHub authentication.

platform_targets.desired_access: ChatVLSC app registration, app roles, consent scopes, redirect URIs, and branding.
platform_targets.current_status: Read verified; write attempt blocked by Authorization_RequestDenied until Application.ReadWrite.All or app admin role is available.

platform_targets.desired_access: Workspace documentation and approval control surface for ChatVLSC configuration.
platform_targets.current_status: Documentation updates are available through the connected Notion workspace.

platform_targets.desired_access: Connector workflow access for Entra, OneDrive, SharePoint, Outlook, To Do, and Notion review actions.
platform_targets.current_status: Connector usage is documented; execution remains gated by workspace credits and explicit authorization for write actions.

platform_targets.desired_access: Organization/workspace console and API access for the primary DNS-domain identity and approved linked IDPs.
platform_targets.current_status: Domain verification records are present; direct console/org membership changes require OpenAI admin console access.

non_goals_for_public_config: Do not publish alternate personal account identifiers to GitHub or public Worker endpoints.
Do not store platform API keys, OAuth client secrets, service tokens, or recovery codes.
Do not grant platform access without platform-native admin approval and audit logging.
